messages.content:
Create a retrieval-oriented summary for this parent section of a document.
Use the child summaries below. Mention the topics, entities, dates, tables,
figures, definitions, procedures, claims, metrics, and evidence types covered
when they are visible. Do not invent facts. Return only the summary text.

Parent:
{
  "node_id": "0017",
  "title": "海外各地区移动游戏收入占比分布",
  "page_index": 20,
  "page_range": "20-21"
}

Child summaries:
[
  {
    "node_id": "0018",
    "title": "美国移动游戏市场收入规模",
    "page_range": "21-22",
    "summary": "**Topics:** US mobile game market revenue trends, market concentration dynamics, player consumption behavior under macroeconomic conditions, strategic resource allocation for game publishers.
**Entities:** US mobile game market, top-tier products (Rank 1–10), mid-tier products (Rank 11–200), players, game developers/publishers, DianDian (data source).
**Dates:** 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025.
**Metrics:** Annual market revenue in 100M RMB (2021: 1740.0; 2022: 1648.6; 2023: 1702.2; 2024: 1634.5; 2025: 1658.4); YoY growth rates (-5.25%, 3.25%, -3.97%, 1.46%); revenue concentration shares by rank for 2023–2025 (e.g., TOP10 at 23.17% in 2024, ~21% in 2023/2025; mid-tier 11–200 expanding to ~53% during growth periods).
**Claims:** Overall market revenue and top-product concentration exhibit a negative correlation. Economic downturns/revenue contraction trigger "safe consumption," increasing top-product market share. Market recovery periods reduce top-product share and expand mid-tier share, driven by differentiated or well-operated titles. Publisher strategy should adapt to macroeconomic forecasts and player risk tolerance.
**Figures/Tables:** Line/bar chart depicting US mobile game market revenue and growth rates (2021–2025); stacked bar chart showing revenue concentration breakdown by product rank (1–10, 11–50, 51–200, others) for 2023–2025.
**Definitions/Procedures:** Defines "safe consumption" as player preference for socially established, low-risk top products during economic uncertainty. Outlines a strategic decision procedure: forecast macroeconomic conditions → assess player safety expectations → choose between deepening existing user value/operational efficiency or launching new products to capture market share.
**Evidence Types:** Quantitative market revenue data,..."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0019",
    "title": "美国移动游戏收入TOP榜",
    "page_range": "22-23",
    "summary": "**Topics:** 2025 US mobile game market revenue rankings; impact of Apple AppStore third-party payment policy changes; top-grossing and new-release mobile games in the US market.

**Entities:** 
- Games: MONOPOLY GO!, Royal Match, 糖果粉碎传奇, Last War: Survival, 金币大师, 无尽冬日, 宝可梦GO, Roblox, 皇室战争, Township, 奔奔王国, Disney Solitaire, 弓箭传说2, 赛马娘（国际服）, DC: Dark Legion, Screwdom, 可狱不可囚, 枫之谷：放置冒险日记, 堡垒之夜, 帕萌战斗日记.
- Developers/Publishers: Scopely, Dream Games, King, First Fun, Moon Active, 点点互动, Niantic, Roblox, Supercell, Playrix, SuperPlay, HABBY, Cygames, FunPlus, Zego, 益世界, NEXON, Epic Games, 莉莉丝.
- Platforms & Sources: Apple App Store, Google Play, 点点数据 (DianDian Data).

**Dates:** Data year: 2025. Policy context: 2025 court ruling. New game launch months: Jan, Feb, Mar, Apr, May, Jun, Oct 2025. Copyright: 2026.

**Claims & Market Analysis:** A 2025 court ruling mandated Apple’s AppStore to open third-party payment channels, shifting the market from “platform-centric” to “service-diversified” by breaking absolute payment binding. Roblox’s traditional channel revenue declined significantly, but overall operations reached new highs due to strategic flexibility from third-party payments. Fortnite’s return to the US AppStore after five years and rapid ascent on new charts confirms the AppStore’s continued dominance as an irreplaceable core traffic hub.

**Tables & Metrics:** 
- Table 1: 2025 US Mobile Game Overall Revenue Top 10 (Columns: Rank, Game Name, Developer, Annual Revenue in 亿元, YoY Growth %). Key metrics: MONOPOLY GO! (75.21亿元, -24.20%), Royal Match (50.94亿元, -14.23%), 皇室战争 (19.10亿元, +120.14%), Roblox (20.66亿元, -50.21%).
- Table 2: 2025 US Mobile Game New Release Revenue Top 10 (Columns: Rank, Game Name, Developer, Annual Revenue in 亿元, Overall Rank, Launch Month). Key metr..."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0020",
    "title": "日本移动游戏市场收入规模",
    "page_range": "23-24",
    "summary": "**Topics:** Japan mobile game market revenue scale, year-over-year growth trends (2021–2025), market revenue concentration by game ranking tiers, regulatory impact on app distribution and payment ecosystems, player monetization drivers.
**Entities:** Japan mobile game market, DianDian (research/data source), 《智能手机特定软件竞争促进法》(Smartphone Specific Software Competition Promotion Act), legacy game titles, voice actors, fan/creator communities, platform operators.
**Dates:** 2021–2025 (market data period), 2025 (primary analysis year), December 18, 2015 (stated effective date of the competition law), year-end (law implementation timeframe), 2026 (report copyright).
**Definitions/Concepts:** App sideloading, third-party payment integration, platform choice screens, revenue concentration tiers (Top 1–10, 11–50, 51–200, Others).
**Procedures/Requirements:** Platform compliance mandates to enable app sideloading, integrate third-party payment systems, and display user choice interfaces per the competition law.
**Claims:** Emotional connection and community belonging outweigh pure gameplay as core monetization drivers; legacy titles sustain high revenue through long-term character narratives, voice acting, fan culture, and community identity; the competition law will fundamentally reshape distribution channels and payment options for developers.
**Tables/Figures:** (1) Time-series chart of Japan mobile game market revenue (亿元/RMB) and YoY growth rates (2021–2025). (2) Stacked chart showing revenue concentration distribution across game ranking tiers (2023–2025).
**Metrics:** 2025 total revenue: 1129.8亿元; 2025 YoY growth: 4.21%; Historical revenue (2021–2024): 1418.6, 1056.5, 1064.1, 1084.2亿元; Historical growth rates: -25.53%, 0.72%, 1.89%; Revenue share ranges by tier (2023–2025):..."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0021",
    "title": "日本移动游戏收入TOP榜",
    "page_range": "24-25",
    "summary": "This section can answer retrieval queries regarding:
- **Topics:** 2025 Japanese mobile game revenue rankings, market competition dynamics ("IP adaptation" vs. "gameplay-driven" models), and player acceptance trends.
- **Entities:** Top-grossing mobile games and their publishers in Japan (e.g., 怪物弹珠/XFLAG, Fate/Grand Order/Aniplex, 宝可梦 TCG Pocket/The Pokemon Company, 崩坏：星穹铁道/米哈游, 无尽冬日/点点互动, SD钢弹 G世代 永恒/万代南梦宫), and the research source (点点数据/DianDian).
- **Dates:** Full-year 2025 revenue tracking period; specific 2025 launch months for new releases (January through October 2025).
- **Definitions:** None provided.
- **Procedures:** None provided.
- **Claims:** The Japanese market exhibits a "dual-track" competition where classic IPs retain strong commercial appeal, while gameplay-innovative titles with high-intensity operations are gaining traction, reflecting increased player acceptance of new mechanics and continuous content updates.
- **Tables:** Two structured ranked lists: (1) 2025 Overall Revenue TOP 10 (columns: Rank, Game Name, Publisher, Annual Revenue, YoY Growth Rate); (2) 2025 New Product Revenue TOP 10 (columns: Rank, Game Name, Publisher, Annual Revenue, Overall Rank, Launch Date).
- **Figures:** None provided.
- **Metrics:** Annual revenue (单位：亿元/100M RMB), year-over-year (YoY) growth rates (%), overall market rank, and new product rank.
- **Evidence Types:** Proprietary quantitative market data (revenue figures, growth percentages, rankings) supplemented by qualitative trend analysis from 点点数据 (DianDian) research."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0022",
    "title": "韩国移动游戏市场收入规模",
    "page_range": "25-26",
    "summary": "**Topics:** South Korean mobile game market revenue scale, market resilience amid socio-economic/political instability, government regulatory policy shifts, publisher market concentration, and IP-driven product strategy.
**Entities:** South Korea, NEXON, Netmarble (网石游戏), South Korean President, DianDian (research source).
**Dates/Periods:** Annual data coverage 2021–2025; specific analytical focus on 2025 market conditions and policy changes.
**Metrics & Figures:** 
- Annual Revenue (RMB 100M): 467.4 (2021), 455.4 (2022), 462.7 (2023), 513.4 (2024), 513.6 (2025).
- YoY Growth Rates: -2.56% (2022), 1.60% (2023), 10.95% (2024), 0.05% (2025).
- Market Concentration: Revenue share percentages for publisher tiers (Top 10, 11–50, 51–200, Others) across 2023–2025.
**Claims & Context:** Despite 2025 domestic turmoil (constitutional crisis, trade shocks, doctor strikes, semiconductor sanctions) and declining consumer confidence, the market maintained slight positive growth. This resilience is attributed to mature player consumption habits and a historic policy shift from strict pre-review controls to active government support and industry endorsement. Major local publishers drove supply by launching classic PC-to-mobile MMORPG adaptations.
**Evidence Types:** Proprietary market research data, time-series revenue and growth charts, market concentration distribution charts, policy/regulatory analysis, and industry trend commentary.
**Answerable Retrieval Questions:** What was the South Korean mobile game market revenue and YoY growth from 2021 to 2025? How did 2025 socio-political events and government policy changes impact market performance? Which companies and product strategies drove 2025 market supply? How is revenue distributed among top, mid-tier, and smaller publishers?..."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0023",
    "title": "韩国移动游戏收入TOP榜",
    "page_range": "26-27",
    "summary": "This section provides a data-driven analysis of the 2025 South Korea mobile game revenue rankings, designed to answer queries on market performance, genre shifts, and top-performing titles. 
**Topics & Claims:** Addresses the historically rare dominance of new releases in the TOP 10 revenue list, noting they are predominantly MMORPGs adapted from classic Korean PC IPs. Claims that intra-company self-competition and new-title success reflect market vitality, while the sharp contraction of casual, idle, and sports genres indicates MMORPG market overconsumption, suggesting untapped potential for other categories over the next 1–2 years. Highlights the entry of Chinese developer Leniu Games’ *I9* into the TOP 10.
**Entities:** Lists top-grossing games (天堂M, 无尽冬日, Last War:Survival, 奥丁：神判, 洛奇M, 吸血鬼, 七骑士 Re:BIRTH, RF ONLINE NEXT, 枫之谷：放置冒险记, I9, 奔奔王国, 尤弥尔传奇, 不休旅途：绘卷世界, 缔造者:放逐之地) and publishers/developers (NCSOFT, 点点互动, First Fun, Kakao Games, NEXON, 网石游戏, 乐牛游戏, 娱美德, 4399, DRIMAGE).
**Dates:** Covers full-year 2025 revenue data; specifies new game launch months from January to October 2025.
**Tables & Metrics:** Features two structured tables: (1) 2025 Overall Revenue TOP 10 (rank, game name, publisher, annual revenue in 亿元, YoY growth rate); (2) 2025 New Release Revenue TOP 10 (rank, game name, publisher, annual revenue, overall rank, launch date). Key metrics include revenue figures, year-over-year growth/decline percentages, and ranking positions.
**Evidence Types:** Combines quantitative financial/ranking data with qualitative market trend analysis, attributed to 点点数据 (Diandian Data) proprietary research.
**Answerable Questions:** What were the top-grossing mobile games in South Korea in 2025? Which new releases and publishers led the revenue chart? How did MMORPGs perform..."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0024",
    "title": "港澳台移动游戏市场收入规模",
    "page_range": "27-28",
    "summary": "Topics & Entities: Hong Kong, Macau, and Taiwan (港澳台) mobile game market; market revenue scale; revenue concentration/competitive landscape; DianDian Data (research source).
Dates: 2021–2025 (annual reporting period).
Metrics: Annual market revenue (RMB billion): 2021 (364.8), 2022 (350.8), 2023 (347.2), 2024 (361.0), 2025 (364.6). YoY growth rates: 2022 (-3.82%), 2023 (-1.05%), 2024 (3.98%), 2025 (0.99%). Revenue concentration by product rank (2023–2025): TOP 10 (~23–24%), 11–50 (26.82% → 28.34%), 51–200 (~25–26%), Others (~23–24%).
Claims & Insights: 2025 revenue nearly matches the 2021 historical peak. The market exhibits a “stable top, strengthened middle” structure, indicating a healthy, dispersed competitive landscape that contrasts with the intensifying top-heavy trends in Japan and Korea. Market resilience is driven by stable player payment habits, diverse genre demand, and consistent content supply, rather than reliance on a few blockbuster titles.
Figures/Tables & Evidence Types: Figure 1: 2021–2025 market revenue scale and YoY growth rate chart. Figure 2: 2023–2025 revenue concentration breakdown by product ranking tiers. Evidence type: Quantitative market data, comparative regional analysis, and proprietary industry research (DianDian Data).
Answerable Retrieval Questions: What was the total mobile game market revenue in HK/Macau/Taiwan from 2021 to 2025? What are the year-over-year growth rates for this market? How is revenue distributed among top, mid-tier, and long-tail mobile games? How does the competitive concentration in this region compare to Japan and Korea? What factors drive market growth and resilience? What specific data supports the “stable top, strengthened middle” claim?"
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0025",
    "title": "港澳台移动游戏收入TOP榜",
    "page_range": "28",
    "summary": "**Topics & Scope:** 2025 mobile game revenue rankings, market composition, and genre preference trends in Hong Kong, Macau, and Taiwan (HKMT).

**Key Claims & Insights:** The export bonus period for lightweight/casual games to the HKMT region is ending. Market preference has explicitly shifted toward mid-heavy games featuring deep gameplay, strong IP backing, or complex progression systems. The market exhibits a mature, tripartite structure comprising established local titles, successful mainland exports (notably SLG), and global IP products.

**Entities (Games & Publishers):** 
- *Top Grossing Titles:* 《星城Online》(Wanin International), 《寒霜启示录》(点点互动), 《最后的战争》(First Fun), 《Garena 传说对决》(Garena), 《杖剑传说》(雷霆游戏), 《天堂W/M》(NCSOFT), 《宝可梦 TCG Pocket》(The Pokemon Company), 《SD钢弹 G世代 永恒》(万代南梦宫), 《崩坏：星穹铁道》(米哈游).
- *Top New Releases (2025):* 《杖剑传说》, 《SD钢弹 G世代 永恒》, 《奔奔王国》(点点互动), 《不休旅途：绘卷世界》(4399), 《枫之谷：放置冒险记》(NEXON), 《七骑士 Re:BIRTH》(网石游戏), 《RO仙境传说：曙光》(GRAVITY), 《时光杂货店》(绝妙游戏M), 《尸鬼三国》(天游网络), 《第七天炎》(Funforge).

**Dates & Timeframes:** Full-year 2025 performance data; specific 2025 launch months for new titles (January, February, March, April, May, July, September, October).

**Tables & Metrics:** 
- *Table 1 (Overall Revenue TOP 10):* Ranks games by 2025 annual revenue (unit: 亿元 RMB) and provides year-over-year growth rates.
- *Table 2 (New Product Revenue TOP 10):* Ranks 2025 releases by revenue, cross-references their overall market rank, and lists launch months.

**Evidence Types:** Quantitative financial metrics (revenue figures, YoY growth percentages), ranked performance lists, qualitative market trend analysis, and publisher/game attribution. Data sourced from 点点数据 (Diandian Data) independent research."
  }
]

message.content:
**Topics:** Overseas mobile game market revenue distribution across the United States, Japan, South Korea, and Hong Kong/Macau/Taiwan (HKMT); regional market scale and year-over-year growth trends (2021–2025); revenue concentration dynamics by product/publisher tiers; 2025 top-grossing and new-release game rankings; impact of macroeconomic conditions, regulatory policies, and platform ecosystem changes on player consumption and publisher strategy.
**Entities:** Regional markets (US, Japan, South Korea, HKMT); top-grossing games (e.g., MONOPOLY GO!, Royal Match, 怪物弹珠, Fate/Grand Order, 天堂M, 星城Online, 崩坏：星穹铁道); publishers/developers (e.g., NEXON, Netmarble, 点点互动, First Fun, miHoYo, Scopely, King); platforms & regulators (Apple AppStore, Google Play, Japan’s Smartphone Specific Software Competition Promotion Act); data source (DianDian Data/点点数据).
**Dates:** 2021–2025 (historical and current market data period); 2025 (primary analysis and ranking year); specific policy timelines (2025 Apple third-party payment court ruling, December 18, 2015 effective date for Japan’s competition law); 2026 (report copyright).
**Figures:** Time-series line/bar charts depicting regional annual market revenue (in 100M RMB/亿元) and YoY growth rates (2021–2025); stacked bar charts illustrating revenue concentration breakdowns by rank tiers (Top 1–10, 11–50, 51–200, Others) for 2023–2025.
**Tables:** Structured ranked lists for each region’s 2025 Overall Revenue TOP 10 and New Release Revenue TOP 10, containing columns for rank, game name, publisher, annual revenue (亿元), YoY growth rate, overall market rank, and launch month.
**Definitions:** "Safe consumption" defined as player preference for socially established, low-risk top-tier products during economic uncertainty; platform compliance requirements including app sideloading, third-party payment integration, and user choice screen displays.
**Procedures:** Strategic decision framework for publishers: forecast macroeconomic conditions → assess player safety expectations/risk tolerance → allocate resources between deepening existing user value/operational efficiency or launching new products to capture mid-tier market share.
**Claims:** Overall market revenue and top-product concentration exhibit a negative correlation; economic downturns trigger safe consumption, increasing top-tier share, while recovery periods expand mid-tier opportunities. Regulatory and platform shifts (Apple payment policy changes, Japan’s competition law, Korea’s policy relaxation) fundamentally reshape distribution and payment ecosystems. Regionally, the US shows macro-driven concentration shifts; Japan relies on IP/emotional monetization and a dual-track IP vs. gameplay competition; South Korea demonstrates MMORPG/IP-driven resilience amid socio-political instability; HKMT exhibits a "stable top, strengthened middle" structure with a clear market shift toward mid-heavy, IP-backed titles.
**Metrics:** Annual market revenue in 100M RMB (US: ~1634–1740; Japan: ~1056–1418; South Korea: ~455–513; HKMT: ~347–364); YoY growth rates (ranging from -25.53% to +10.95% across regions/years); revenue concentration percentages by tier (2023–2025); individual game annual revenues and YoY growth/decline percentages for 2025 rankings.
**Evidence Types:** Proprietary quantitative market revenue data, YoY growth metrics, ranked financial tables, time-series and stacked concentration charts, policy/regulatory analysis, qualitative industry trend commentary, and comparative regional market analysis sourced from DianDian Data research.
message.reasoning_content:
Here's a thinking process:

1.  **Analyze User Input:**
    - **Task:** Create a retrieval-oriented summary for a parent section of a document.
    - **Input Data:** Parent section info (node_id: 0017, title: "海外各地区移动游戏收入占比分布", page_index: 20, page_range: 20-21) and 8 child summaries (nodes 0018-0025).
    - **Requirements:** Mention topics, entities, dates, tables, figures, definitions, procedures, claims, metrics, and evidence types when visible. Do not invent facts. Return only the summary text.
    - **Child Summaries Content:**
        - 0018: US market revenue scale (2021-2025), concentration, safe consumption, macroeconomic impact, charts/tables.
        - 0019: US market TOP revenue list (2025), Apple AppStore policy change, top games/publishers, tables.
        - 0020: Japan market revenue scale (2021-2025), competition law impact, emotional/community monetization, charts.
        - 0021: Japan market TOP revenue list (2025), IP vs gameplay, dual-track competition, tables.
        - 0022: South Korea market revenue scale (2021-2025), socio-political resilience, policy shift, IP-driven MMORPGs, charts.
        - 0023: South Korea market TOP revenue list (2025), MMORPG dominance, new releases, tables.
        - 0024: HK/Macau/Taiwan market revenue scale (2021-2025), "stable top, strengthened middle", charts.
        - 0025: HK/Macau/Taiwan market TOP revenue list (2025), shift to mid-heavy games, tripartite structure, tables.
    - **Common Themes:** All children cover specific overseas regions (US, Japan, South Korea, HKMT). Each region has two parts: market revenue scale/trends (with concentration metrics, charts, claims about macro/policy/player behavior) and TOP revenue rankings (with tables of top 10 overall & new releases, publisher/game entities, genre/IP trends). Data spans 2021-2025, with 2025 as the primary focus. Source is consistently DianDian Data.

2.  **Identify Key Elements for Parent Summary:**
    - **Topics:** Overseas mobile game market revenue distribution across regions (US, Japan, South Korea, HK/Macau/Taiwan); market scale & YoY growth trends (2021–2025); revenue concentration by product/publisher tiers; regional TOP revenue rankings (overall & new releases); impact of macroeconomic conditions, regulatory policies, and platform changes; genre/IP trends and player consumption behavior.
    - **Entities:** Regions (US, Japan, South Korea, HKMT); Games & Publishers (e.g., MONOPOLY GO!, Royal Match, 怪物弹珠, Fate/Grand Order, 天堂M, 星城Online, NEXON, Netmarble, 点点互动, First Fun, miHoYo, etc.); Platforms/Regulators (Apple AppStore, Google Play, Smartphone Specific Software Competition Promotion Act); Data Source (DianDian Data/点点数据).
    - **Dates:** 2021–2025 (historical & current market data); 2025 (primary analysis & ranking year); specific policy dates (e.g., 2025 court ruling, Dec 2015 law effective date); 2026 (report copyright).
    - **Tables:** Regional TOP 10 Overall Revenue tables and TOP 10 New Release Revenue tables for each region (US, Japan, South Korea, HKMT), containing rank, game name, publisher, annual revenue (亿元 RMB), YoY growth, and launch months.
    - **Figures:** Time-series line/bar charts for regional market revenue & YoY growth (2021–2025); stacked bar charts for revenue concentration distribution by rank tiers (Top 1–10, 11–50, 51–200, Others) across 2023–2025.
    - **Definitions/Procedures:** "Safe consumption" (preference for established top products during economic uncertainty); platform compliance procedures (sideloading, third-party payments, choice screens per competition law); strategic decision procedure (forecast macro → assess player risk → allocate resources).
    - **Claims:** Market revenue and top-product concentration show negative correlation; economic downturns boost top-tier share via safe consumption, while recovery expands mid-tier share; regulatory shifts (Apple payment policy, Japan competition law, Korea policy relaxation) reshape distribution/payment ecosystems; regional markets exhibit distinct structures (US: macro-driven concentration shifts; Japan: IP/emotional monetization & dual-track competition; Korea: MMORPG/IP dominance & resilience amid turmoil; HKMT: "stable top, strengthened middle" & shift to mid-heavy games); genre preferences are evolving globally.
    - **Metrics:** Annual market revenue (100M RMB/亿元) and YoY growth rates per region (2021–2025); revenue concentration percentages by tier (2023–2025); individual game annual revenues and YoY growth/rankings for 2025.
    - **Evidence Types:** Quantitative market revenue data, YoY growth metrics, ranked financial tables, time-series & stacked concentration charts, proprietary industry research from DianDian Data, qualitative trend analysis, and policy/regulatory documentation.

3.  **Draft Summary (Mental Refinement - ensuring retrieval-oriented structure & strict adherence to constraints):**
    This section provides a comprehensive, retrieval-oriented overview of mobile game revenue distribution across key overseas markets (United States, Japan, South Korea, and Hong Kong/Macau/Taiwan) from 2021 to 2025, with 2025 as the primary analytical focus. It covers regional market revenue scales, year-over-year growth trends, revenue concentration dynamics, and top-grossing game rankings. Key entities include regional markets, major publishers (e.g., NEXON, Netmarble, 点点互动, First Fun, miHoYo), top-performing titles (e.g., MONOPOLY GO!, Royal Match, 怪物弹珠, 天堂M, 星城Online), platform regulators (Apple AppStore, Google Play), and the data source DianDian Data (点点数据). The analysis spans 2021–2025, referencing specific policy timelines (e.g., 2025 Apple third-party payment ruling, Japan’s Smartphone Specific Software Competition Promotion Act) and a 2026 report copyright.

    The section features time-series line/bar charts tracking annual market revenue (in 100M RMB/亿元) and YoY growth rates, alongside stacked bar charts illustrating revenue concentration breakdowns by product/publisher tiers (Top 1–10, 11–50, 51–200, Others) for 2023–2025. It includes structured tables for each region’s 2025 Overall Revenue TOP 10 and New Release Revenue TOP 10, detailing rank, game name, publisher, annual revenue, YoY growth, and launch months.

    Core claims highlight that market revenue and top-product concentration exhibit a negative correlation, with economic downturns triggering "safe consumption" (defined as player preference for established, low-risk titles) that boosts top-tier share, while recovery periods expand mid-tier opportunities. Regulatory and platform shifts (e.g., Apple’s payment policy changes, Japan’s app sideloading/third-party payment mandates, Korea’s transition from strict pre-review to industry support) are shown to fundamentally reshape distribution and monetization. Regionally, the US market demonstrates macro-driven concentration shifts; Japan relies on IP/emotional monetization and a "dual-track" IP vs. gameplay competition; South Korea shows MMORPG/IP-driven resilience amid socio-political instability; and HKMT exhibits a "stable top, strengthened middle" structure with a clear shift toward mid-heavy, IP-backed titles. Strategic procedures outlined include forecasting macroeconomic conditions, assessing player risk tolerance, and aligning publisher resource allocation accordingly.

    Metrics covered include regional annual revenues (e.g., US ~1634–1740, Japan ~1056–1418, Korea ~455–513, HKMT ~347–364 in 100M RMB), YoY growth rates, tier-based revenue concentration percentages, and individual game financial performance. Evidence types consist of proprietary quantitative market data, ranked financial tables, time-series and concentration charts, policy/regulatory analysis, and qualitative industry trend commentary sourced from DianDian Data research.

4.  **Check against Constraints:**
    - *Retrieval-oriented summary?* Yes, structured to answer queries about regions, metrics, trends, tables, claims, etc.
    - *Mention topics, entities, dates, tables, figures, definitions, procedures, claims, metrics, evidence types?* All explicitly covered.
    - *Do not invent facts?* Strictly derived from child summaries. Numbers and names match.
    - *Return only the summary text?* Yes.
    - *Parent context:* Matches "海外各地区移动游戏收入占比分布" (Overseas regional mobile game revenue distribution).
    - *Format:* Plain text, cohesive paragraph(s) or structured but without markdown headers if possible, or just standard text. I'll keep it concise and well-structured for retrieval.

    I will refine it to ensure it flows well as a single retrieval summary, explicitly tagging or weaving in the required elements naturally but clearly. I'll make sure all constraints are met precisely.

    Structure:
    - Overview/Topics & Dates
    - Entities & Data Sources
    - Figures & Tables
    - Definitions & Procedures
    - Claims & Regional Insights
    - Metrics & Evidence Types
    (Will combine into a cohesive paragraph/block as requested, but ensure all keywords are present.)

    Let's draft carefully.

    **Entities:** Regional markets (US, Japan, South Korea, HKMT); top-grossing games (e.g., MONOPOLY GO!, Royal Match, 怪物弹珠, Fate/Grand Order, 天堂M, 星城Online, 崩坏：星穹铁道); publishers/developers (e.g., NEXON, Netmarble, 点点互动, First Fun, miHoYo, Scopely, King); platforms & regulators (Apple AppStore, Google Play, Japan’s Smartphone Specific Software Competition Promotion Act); data source (DianDian Data/点点数据).

    - **Child Summaries Content:**
        - 0018: US market revenue scale (2021-2025), concentration, safe consumption, macroeconomic impact, charts/tables.
        - 0019: US market TOP revenue list (2025), Apple AppStore policy change, top games/publishers, tables.
        - 0020: Japan market revenue scale (2021-2025), competition law impact, emotional/community monetization, charts.
        - 0021: Japan market TOP revenue list (2025), IP vs gameplay, dual-track competition, tables.
        - 0022: South Korea market revenue scale (2021-2025), socio-political resilience, policy shift, IP-driven MMORPGs, charts.
        - 0023: South Korea market TOP revenue list (2025), MMORPG dominance, new releases, tables.
        - 0024: HK/Macau/Taiwan market revenue scale (2021-2025), "stable top, strengthened middle", charts.
        - 0025: HK/Macau/Taiwan market TOP revenue list (2025), shift to mid-heavy games, tripartite structure, tables.
    - **Common Themes:** All children cover specific overseas regions (US, Japan, South Korea, HKMT). Each region has two parts: market revenue scale/trends (with concentration metrics, charts, claims about macro/policy/player behavior) and TOP revenue rankings (with tables of top 10 overall & new releases, publisher/game entities, genre/IP trends). Data spans 2021-2025, with 2025 as the primary focus. Source is consistently DianDian Data.

2.  **Identify Key Elements for Parent Summary:**
    - **Topics:** Overseas mobile game market revenue distribution across regions (US, Japan, South Korea, HK/Macau/Taiwan); market scale & YoY growth trends (2021–2025); revenue concentration by product/publisher tiers; regional TOP revenue rankings (overall & new releases); impact of macroeconomic conditions, regulatory policies, and platform changes; genre/IP trends and player consumption behavior.
    - **Entities:** Regions (US, Japan, South Korea, HKMT); Games & Publishers (e.g., MONOPOLY GO!, Royal Match, 怪物弹珠, Fate/Grand Order, 天堂M, 星城Online, NEXON, Netmarble, 点点互动, First Fun, miHoYo, etc.); Platforms/Regulators (Apple AppStore, Google Play, Smartphone Specific Software Competition Promotion Act); Data Source (DianDian Data/点点数据).
    - **Dates:** 2021–2025 (historical & current market data); 2025 (primary analysis & ranking year); specific policy dates (e.g., 2025 court ruling, Dec 2015 law effective date); 2026 (report copyright).
    - **Tables:** Regional TOP 10 Overall Revenue tables and TOP 10 New Release Revenue tables for each region (US, Japan, South Korea, HKMT), containing rank, game name, publisher, annual revenue (亿元 RMB), YoY growth, and launch months.
    - **Figures:** Time-series line/bar charts for regional market revenue & YoY growth (2021–2025); stacked bar charts for revenue concentration distribution by rank tiers (Top 1–10, 11–50, 51–200, Others) across 2023–2025.
    - **Definitions/Procedures:** "Safe consumption" (preference for established top products during economic uncertainty); platform compliance procedures (sideloading, third-party payments, choice screens per competition law); strategic decision procedure (forecast macro → assess player risk → allocate resources).
    - **Claims:** Market revenue and top-product concentration show negative correlation; economic downturns boost top-tier share via safe consumption, while recovery expands mid-tier share; regulatory shifts (Apple payment policy, Japan competition law, Korea policy relaxation) reshape distribution/payment ecosystems; regional markets exhibit distinct structures (US: macro-driven concentration shifts; Japan: IP/emotional monetization & dual-track competition; Korea: MMORPG/IP dominance & resilience amid turmoil; HKMT: "stable top, strengthened middle" & shift to mid-heavy games); genre preferences are evolving globally.
    - **Metrics:** Annual market revenue (100M RMB/亿元) and YoY growth rates per region (2021–2025); revenue concentration percentages by tier (2023–2025); individual game annual revenues and YoY growth/rankings for 2025.
    - **Evidence Types:** Quantitative market revenue data, YoY growth metrics, ranked financial tables, time-series & stacked concentration charts, proprietary industry research from DianDian Data, qualitative trend analysis, and policy/regulatory documentation.

3.  **Draft Summary (Mental Refinement - ensuring retrieval-oriented structure & strict adherence to constraints):**
    This section provides a comprehensive, retrieval-oriented overview of mobile game revenue distribution across key overseas markets (United States, Japan, South Korea, and Hong Kong/Macau/Taiwan) from 2021 to 2025, with 2025 as the primary analytical focus. It covers regional market revenue scales, year-over-year growth trends, revenue concentration dynamics, and top-grossing game rankings. Key entities include regional markets, major publishers (e.g., NEXON, Netmarble, 点点互动, First Fun, miHoYo), top-performing titles (e.g., MONOPOLY GO!, Royal Match, 怪物弹珠, 天堂M, 星城Online), platform regulators (Apple AppStore, Google Play), and the data source DianDian Data (点点数据). The analysis spans 2021–2025, referencing specific policy timelines (e.g., 2025 Apple third-party payment ruling, Japan’s Smartphone Specific Software Competition Promotion Act) and a 2026 report copyright.

    The section features time-series line/bar charts tracking annual market revenue (in 100M RMB/亿元) and YoY growth rates, alongside stacked bar charts illustrating revenue concentration breakdowns by product/publisher tiers (Top 1–10, 11–50, 51–200, Others) for 2023–2025. It includes structured tables for each region’s 2025 Overall Revenue TOP 10 and New Release Revenue TOP 10, detailing rank, game name, publisher, annual revenue, YoY growth, and launch months.

    Core claims highlight that market revenue and top-product concentration exhibit a negative correlation, with economic downturns triggering "safe consumption" (defined as player preference for established, low-risk titles) that boosts top-tier share, while recovery periods expand mid-tier opportunities. Regulatory and platform shifts (e.g., Apple’s payment policy changes, Japan’s app sideloading/third-party payment mandates, Korea’s transition from strict pre-review to industry support) are shown to fundamentally reshape distribution and monetization. Regionally, the US market demonstrates macro-driven concentration shifts; Japan relies on IP/emotional monetization and a "dual-track" IP vs. gameplay competition; South Korea shows MMORPG/IP-driven resilience amid socio-political instability; and HKMT exhibits a "stable top, strengthened middle" structure with a clear shift toward mid-heavy, IP-backed titles. Strategic procedures outlined include forecasting macroeconomic conditions, assessing player risk tolerance, and aligning publisher resource allocation accordingly.

    Metrics covered include regional annual revenues (e.g., US ~1634–1740, Japan ~1056–1418, Korea ~455–513, HKMT ~347–364 in 100M RMB), YoY growth rates, tier-based revenue concentration percentages, and individual game financial performance. Evidence types consist of proprietary quantitative market data, ranked financial tables, time-series and concentration charts, policy/regulatory analysis, and qualitative industry trend commentary sourced from DianDian Data research.

response_text:
**Topics:** Overseas mobile game market revenue distribution across the United States, Japan, South Korea, and Hong Kong/Macau/Taiwan (HKMT); regional market scale and year-over-year growth trends (2021–2025); revenue concentration dynamics by product/publisher tiers; 2025 top-grossing and new-release game rankings; impact of macroeconomic conditions, regulatory policies, and platform ecosystem changes on player consumption and publisher strategy.
**Entities:** Regional markets (US, Japan, South Korea, HKMT); top-grossing games (e.g., MONOPOLY GO!, Royal Match, 怪物弹珠, Fate/Grand Order, 天堂M, 星城Online, 崩坏：星穹铁道); publishers/developers (e.g., NEXON, Netmarble, 点点互动, First Fun, miHoYo, Scopely, King); platforms & regulators (Apple AppStore, Google Play, Japan’s Smartphone Specific Software Competition Promotion Act); data source (DianDian Data/点点数据).
**Dates:** 2021–2025 (historical and current market data period); 2025 (primary analysis and ranking year); specific policy timelines (2025 Apple third-party payment court ruling, December 18, 2015 effective date for Japan’s competition law); 2026 (report copyright).
**Figures:** Time-series line/bar charts depicting regional annual market revenue (in 100M RMB/亿元) and YoY growth rates (2021–2025); stacked bar charts illustrating revenue concentration breakdowns by rank tiers (Top 1–10, 11–50, 51–200, Others) for 2023–2025.
**Tables:** Structured ranked lists for each region’s 2025 Overall Revenue TOP 10 and New Release Revenue TOP 10, containing columns for rank, game name, publisher, annual revenue (亿元), YoY growth rate, overall market rank, and launch month.
**Definitions:** "Safe consumption" defined as player preference for socially established, low-risk top-tier products during economic uncertainty; platform compliance requirements including app sideloading, third-party payment integration, and user choice screen displays.
**Procedures:** Strategic decision framework for publishers: forecast macroeconomic conditions → assess player safety expectations/risk tolerance → allocate resources between deepening existing user value/operational efficiency or launching new products to capture mid-tier market share.
**Claims:** Overall market revenue and top-product concentration exhibit a negative correlation; economic downturns trigger safe consumption, increasing top-tier share, while recovery periods expand mid-tier opportunities. Regulatory and platform shifts (Apple payment policy changes, Japan’s competition law, Korea’s policy relaxation) fundamentally reshape distribution and payment ecosystems. Regionally, the US shows macro-driven concentration shifts; Japan relies on IP/emotional monetization and a dual-track IP vs. gameplay competition; South Korea demonstrates MMORPG/IP-driven resilience amid socio-political instability; HKMT exhibits a "stable top, strengthened middle" structure with a clear market shift toward mid-heavy, IP-backed titles.
**Metrics:** Annual market revenue in 100M RMB (US: ~1634–1740; Japan: ~1056–1418; South Korea: ~455–513; HKMT: ~347–364); YoY growth rates (ranging from -25.53% to +10.95% across regions/years); revenue concentration percentages by tier (2023–2025); individual game annual revenues and YoY growth/decline percentages for 2025 rankings.
**Evidence Types:** Proprietary quantitative market revenue data, YoY growth metrics, ranked financial tables, time-series and stacked concentration charts, policy/regulatory analysis, qualitative industry trend commentary, and comparative regional market analysis sourced from DianDian Data research.
